author: nc1234
content:
> Adding a new contract with pending audit and review/improvement loops will take some time
I am not sure how much time it takes to audit the contract (I have zero knowledge about this), but afaik it should be very similar to 7002 contracts. Also arguably, the spec is simplified nicely, no more validator and builder flow clustered together, and a lot of `if (builder) do this elif (validator) do this` statements are removed.

author: pk910
content: well,  the proposed contract is written in solidity, not asm and reusing the queue logic.  So I'd threat that as a completely new audit  of a new codebase.. But might be wrong

author: nc1234
content: https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11760 it is rewritten in eas, to align more closely with 7002 contracts.

author: pk910
content: oh yea, that changed since I last saw that proposal o.O  thx for the hint
